title: The Lost and Found Room
learningGoal: The learner will decode dense clusters of animate genitives (Anna's), inanimate partitives (Back of the room), and spatial demonstratives (This/Those) within a comparative narrative context.
instructions: Read the story about a teacher looking at lost items in her school. Pay very close attention to HOW she talks about who owns what. Look for the apostrophe 'S' attached to names, and notice when she uses 'This' versus 'These'. Answer the 8 questions below.

contentItems.text: Remember: 'This' is for one thing close to her. 'These' is for many things close to her. 'Those' is for many things far away!

readingText: Mrs. Sharma is a teacher. She is standing in the Lost and Found room of the school. The room is full of things. She picks up a red jacket. 'This is Amit's jacket,' she says. 'He lost it yesterday.' Then, she looks at two blue bags on the floor near her feet. 'These are the girls' bags. This is Neha's bag, and this is Priya's bag.' She walks to the back of the room. She points at a bicycle near the window. 'That is Rahul's bicycle. The tire of the bicycle is flat.' Next to the bicycle are five green umbrellas. 'Those are the teachers' umbrellas,' she says. Mrs. Sharma sees a small dog sitting near the door of the room. 'Wow! This is the principal's dog. What is it doing here?' she laughs. She gives the dog some water. The color of the dog is black and white. Mrs. Sharma has a lot of work to do today to return all these things to the students.
textInTargetLanguage: Mrs. Sharma is a teacher. She is standing in the Lost and Found room of the school. The room is full of things. She picks up a red jacket. 'This is Amit's jacket,' she says. 'He lost it yesterday.' Then, she looks at two blue bags on the floor near her feet. 'These are the girls' bags. This is Neha's bag, and this is Priya's bag.' She walks to the back of the room. She points at a bicycle near the window. 'That is Rahul's bicycle. The tire of the bicycle is flat.' Next to the bicycle are five green umbrellas. 'Those are the teachers' umbrellas,' she says. Mrs. Sharma sees a small dog sitting near the door of the room. 'Wow! This is the principal's dog. What is it doing here?' she laughs. She gives the dog some water. The color of the dog is black and white. Mrs. Sharma has a lot of work to do today to return all these things to the students.
baseLanguageSupportText: श्रीमती शर्मा एक शिक्षिका हैं। वह स्कूल के 'लॉस्ट एंड फाउंड' (खोया-पाया) कमरे में खड़ी हैं। कमरा चीजों से भरा है। वह एक लाल जैकेट उठाती हैं। 'यह अमित की जैकेट है,' वह कहती हैं। 'उसने इसे कल खो दिया।' फिर, वह अपने पैरों के पास फर्श पर दो नीले बैग देखती हैं। 'ये लड़कियों के बैग हैं। यह नेहा का बैग है, और यह प्रिया का बैग है।' वह कमरे के पीछे की ओर जाती हैं। वह खिड़की के पास एक साइकिल की ओर इशारा करती हैं। 'वह राहुल की साइकिल है। साइकिल का टायर सपाट (हवा निकली हुई) है।' साइकिल के आगे पांच हरे रंग के छाते हैं। 'वे शिक्षकों के छाते हैं,' वह कहती हैं। श्रीमती शर्मा कमरे के दरवाजे के पास एक छोटा कुत्ता बैठा देखती हैं। 'वाह! यह प्रिंसिपल का कुत्ता है। यह यहाँ क्या कर रहा है?' वह हंसती हैं। वह कुत्ते को थोड़ा पानी देती हैं। कुत्ते का रंग काला और सफेद है। श्रीमती शर्मा को आज इन सभी चीजों को छात्रों को वापस करने के लिए बहुत काम करना है।

exampleSentence.targetText: She walks to the back of the room.
exampleSentence.transliteration: शी वाक्स टू द बैक ऑफ द रूम।
exampleSentence.baseTranslation: वह कमरे के पीछे जाती है।

exampleSentence.targetText: Near the door of the room.
exampleSentence.transliteration: नियर द डोर ऑफ द रूम।

sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: यह अमित की जैकेट है। (Uses 'This' because she is holding it)

sentenceSupportPairs.targetText: The tire of the bicycle is flat.
sentenceSupportPairs.transliteration: द टायर ऑफ द बाइसिकल इज़ फ्लैट।
sentenceSupportPairs.baseTranslation: साइकिल का टायर सपाट है। (Uses 'Of' because a bicycle is an object)
वे शिक्षकों के छाते हैं। (Uses 'Those' because there are 5 umbrellas far away)

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Why does she say 'These are the girls' bags' instead of 'This are the girls' bags'?
options: Because there is more than one bag

comprehensionQuestions.questionText: Find the description of the bicycle. Does the text say 'The bicycle's tire' or 'The tire of the bicycle'?
What word does she use to point at the five umbrellas far away?
Write the exact three words used to say 'कमरे का दरवाज़ा'.

correctAnswers.q2: Because there is more than one bag

expectedSummaryPoints: The learner correlates the apostrophe 'S' to human/animate ownership (Amit's jacket, principal's dog).
The learner recognizes 'Of' acting as the possessive connector for inanimate complexes (tire of the bicycle).

feedbackRules.onPass: Magnificent! You perfectly decoded the ownership rules. You easily identified who owned what by looking for the Apostrophe 'S'!
feedbackRules.onFail: Keep trying! Remember, when a PERSON owns something, look for their name followed by an 'S. Amit's jacket. Rahul's bicycle.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort! Watch out for the 'Of' rule. We say 'Amit's jacket', but we do NOT say 'bicycle's tire'. We say 'The tire OF the bicycle'.

audioAssets.audioTranscript: Mrs. Sharma is a teacher... (full transcript provided in text)

hintText: Pay close attention to where the teacher is pointing! If she says 'This', it is in her hand. If she says 'Those', she is pointing across the room.
commonMistakes: Thinking 'girls'' means one girl, failing to notice the apostrophe after the S.
